label: PLA Ballistic Missile — Aug 2022 (Northern Arc)
damage: Landed in Japan EEZ; no casualties
notes: Five Dongfeng-15B/17 missiles flew over northern Taiwan and landed in Japan's EEZ; formal protest by Tokyo

label: PLA Ballistic Missile — Aug 2022 (Southern Arc)
damage: Landed in designated exercise zones east/southeast of Taiwan
notes: Dongfeng-15B series fired into eastern exercise zones; 11 total missiles in Aug 2022 exercises

label: PLA Continuous Strait Patrol Route
notes: Post-Pelosi 'new normal' PLA patrols in Taiwan Strait; near-daily operations since Aug 2022

label: USS Ronald Reagan — Philippine Sea Positioning
notes: CVN-76 repositioned from South China Sea to Philippine Sea east of Taiwan during Aug 2022 PLA exercises; deliberate deterrent positioning without direct strait transit

label: USS Higgins / Antietam Strait Transit
notes: First dual-ship US Navy transit of Taiwan Strait post-Pelosi crisis; destroyer + cruiser; PLA ships shadowed closely

label: PLA ADIZ Incursion — Northern Sector
notes: 21 PLA aircraft entered ADIZ on Aug 2; 22 crossed median line same day; record monthly total of 446 in Aug 2022

label: PLA ADIZ Incursion — Southwest Sector
notes: Frequent southwest ADIZ incursion corridor; PLAAF bombers H-6K and J-16 fighters most common aircraft type

label: Joint Sword-2024A — Northern Blockade Arc
notes: 46 PLA naval vessels deployed in cordon around Taiwan May 23-24, 2024; largest PLAN maritime deployment recorded

label: Joint Sword-2024A — Eastern Cordon
notes: Eastern naval cordon in Joint Sword-2024A; covered Taiwan's Pacific coast access; anti-submarine and anti-surface warfare drills

label: Justice Mission-2025 — Centerline Mass Crossing
notes: 90 of 130 PLA aircraft sorties crossed Taiwan Strait centerline in Justice Mission-2025; largest single-exercise crossing event recorded

label: Justice Mission-2025 — Contiguous Zone Operations
notes: 11 PLAN coast guard vessels operated in Taiwan's 12-24 nm contiguous zone; unprecedented legal-military hybrid blockade rehearsal

label: Strait Thunder-2025A — Rocket Artillery Fire
notes: First missile/rocket fires into Taiwan Strait since Aug 2022; landed near 24 nm contiguous zone boundary; April 2025

label: B-52 Bomber Patrol Route (Guam → Kadena)
notes: USAF B-52 Stratofortress strategic bomber patrol routes from Andersen AFB Guam through Okinawa theater; regular deterrence flights 2022-2026

label: P-8 Poseidon Maritime Patrol — Taiwan Strait
notes: US Navy P-8 Poseidon flights monitoring PLA naval movements in and around Taiwan Strait; near-daily operations during exercises

label: PLA Drone Surveillance — Kinmen/Matsu Corridor
notes: PLA UAV overflights of Taiwan-controlled Kinmen and Matsu islands; near-weekly events from late 2022; Taiwan unable to establish clear legal basis to fire

label: US Arms Delivery Route — Taiwan
notes: Conceptual arms supply route; US DSCA approved $39B+ in arms to Taiwan 2015-2025 including F-16 munitions, HIMARS, Harpoon missiles, Patriot upgrades

label: Joint Sword-2024B — Blockade Scenario
notes: Oct 14, 2024 exercise focused on legal/gray-zone blockade; CCG vessels integrated with PLAN for first time in major exercise

label: Japan-US Security Coordination
notes: Japan-US security coordination corridor; Japan formally protested PLA missiles landing in EEZ Aug 2022; later announced defense budget doubling

label: PLA Feb 2025 Drill — West Taiwan Coast
notes: PLA joint combat readiness drill 40 nm off Taiwan's southwest coast; Feb 26, 2025; fighter jets, UAVs, surface warships in coordinated formation

label: US-Philippines Access Route (EDCA Bases)
notes: US gained access to 4 additional Philippine bases under EDCA 2023 expansion; key for Taiwan contingency southern approach; reduces Guam dependency

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW Sector (Mar 20, 2026)
notes: 4 of 6 PLA sorties crossed median line into SW and eastern ADIZ on March 20, 2026; 8 PLAN vessels also operating; moderate post-NPC activity level

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Eastern Sector (Mar 21, 2026)
notes: 1 of 2 PLA sorties crossed median line into eastern ADIZ on March 21, 2026; 8 PLAN vessels operating; minimal air activity day

label: PLA ADIZ Mass Crossing — SW/East (Mar 25, 2026)
notes: 13 of 16 PLA sorties crossed median line into SW and eastern ADIZ on March 25, 2026; 10 PLAN vessels + 2 official ships; single-day spike amid elevated tensions preceding rescheduled Trump-Xi summit

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW/East (Mar 27, 2026)
notes: 4 of 6 PLA sorties crossed median line into SW and eastern ADIZ on March 27, 2026; 10 PLAN ships + 2 official ships; activity returned to moderate after Mar 25 spike

label: PLA ADIZ Mass Crossing — 4 Sectors (Mar 28, 2026)
notes: 11 of 15 PLA sorties crossed median line into northern, central, southwestern and eastern ADIZ on March 28, 2026; aircraft types: J-10, J-16 fighters and KJ-500 AEW&C; 7 PLAN vessels + 2 official ships; PLA described activity as 'air-sea joint training'; multi-sector crossing for first time since March 18

label: PLA ADIZ Peak Crossing — 4 Sectors (Mar 29, 2026)
notes: 13 of 19 PLA sorties crossed median line into northern, central, southwestern and eastern ADIZ on March 29, 2026 — highest single-day count of the late-March spike; 9 PLAN vessels + 2 official ships; PLA Eastern Theater described as 'air-sea joint training'; three-day escalation: 6 (Mar 27) → 15 (Mar 28) → 19 (Mar 29)

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW/SE Sectors (Mar 30, 2026)
notes: 1 of 1 PLA sorties crossed median line into SW/SE ADIZ on March 30, 2026; sharp drop from prior-day peak of 19 sorties; 9 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship; return to baseline after late-March spike; Taiwan deployed CAP, Navy, and coastal missiles in response

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Southern Sector (Mar 31, 2026)
notes: 1 of 1 PLA sorties entered southern ADIZ on March 31, 2026; 6 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship — continuing de-escalation from 11 peak vessels on Mar 29; second consecutive minimal-activity day confirms late-March spike was bounded, not sustained campaign

label: PLA ADIZ Mass Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 1, 2026)
notes: 11 of 11 PLA sorties entered SW ADIZ on April 1, 2026; 7 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship; sudden spike from 1-sortie baseline of Mar 30-31 — reverses post-peak de-escalation; coincides with KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun confirming April 7-12 China visit; consistent with PLA combined pressure-and-dialogue signaling pattern

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW/East Sectors (Apr 3, 2026)
notes: 2 of 2 PLA sorties entered SW and eastern ADIZ on April 3, 2026; 7 PLAN vessels + 2 official ships; de-escalation from 11 sorties on April 1; oscillating volatile pattern since Mar 29 peak (19 sorties); PLA warship warned US P-8 Poseidon near Hengchun Peninsula at 0647 local time same day

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 5, 2026)
notes: 3 of 3 PLA sorties crossed Taiwan Strait median line into SW ADIZ on April 5, 2026; slight uptick from 2 sorties on April 3; YTD 2026 PLA sorties down 46.5% vs same period 2025 (460 sorties YTD vs. comparable 2025 figure); pattern shows maintained low-level presence 2 days before KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun's April 7 China departure

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 7, 2026)
notes: 1 PLA aircraft sortie entered SW ADIZ as of 0600 local Apr 7, 2026; 8 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship operating around Taiwan in multi-vector posture (east coast, northwest, southwest); minimal air activity on day of KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun's arrival in Shanghai — consistent with PLA combined pressure-and-dialogue signaling; April running total: 205 aircraft tracks, 294 ship tracks

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Northern + SE Sectors (Apr 8, 2026)
notes: 2 PLA aircraft sorties (both crossing median line) entered northern and southeastern ADIZ sectors as of 0600 local Apr 8, 2026; uptick from 1 sortie Apr 7; 9 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship in multi-vector posture; northern sector incursion is first since late-March spike; coincides with Day 2 of KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun's China visit (Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, Nanjing); combined pressure-and-dialogue signaling pattern continues

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Northern + SW Sectors (Apr 9, 2026)
notes: 6 PLA aircraft sorties detected as of 0600 local Apr 9, 2026; 5 of 6 crossed the median line into northern and southwestern ADIZ sectors; 8 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship operating around Taiwan in multi-vector posture; significant uptick from 2 sorties on Apr 8; coincides with Day 3 of KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun's visit — delegation traveling Nanjing→Beijing ahead of confirmed Apr 10 Xi meeting; PLA sustained elevated pressure-and-dialogue signaling pattern

label: KMT Delegation — Shanghai to Beijing (Apr 7–10, 2026)
notes: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's delegation traveled from Shanghai (Apr 7) → Nanjing (Apr 8, Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum) → Beijing (Apr 9-10) for historic Xi Jinping meeting at Great Hall of the People Apr 10; first CPC-KMT leader summit in ~10 years; 34 days before Trump-Xi summit May 14-15

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Northern + SW Sectors (Apr 10, 2026)
notes: 7 PLA aircraft sorties detected as of 0600 local Apr 10, 2026; all 7 crossed the median line into northern and southwestern ADIZ sectors; 7 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship operating around Taiwan; PLA sustained elevated air pressure on the same day as the Xi–KMT summit at the Great Hall of the People; uptick from 6 sorties (Apr 9); confirms PLA pressure-and-dialogue dual-track strategy

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Northern + SW Sectors (Apr 11, 2026)
notes: 17 PLA aircraft sorties detected as of 0600 local Apr 11, 2026; 15 of 17 crossed the median line into northern and southwestern ADIZ sectors; 7 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship in multi-vector posture; highest single-day sortie count since Mar 29 peak (19 sorties); dramatic post-Xi-KMT summit surge — day after Cheng Li-wun met Xi at Great Hall of the People; PLA signaling that political dialogue does not constrain military pressure on Taiwan's DPP government; Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles in response

label: JMSDF Destroyer Ikazuchi — Taiwan Strait Transit (Apr 17, 2026)
notes: First JMSDF destroyer transit through Taiwan Strait under PM Takaichi; transited 04:02–17:50 local; China condemned as 'deliberate provocation'; vessel headed to South China Sea for US-Philippines Balikatan exercises; marks Japan joining US freedom-of-navigation operations in the strait

label: PLA Eastern Theater Joint Combat Readiness Patrols — East China Sea (Apr 18, 2026)
notes: PLA Eastern Theater Command 'joint combat readiness patrols' in East China Sea following JMSDF destroyer transit; Type 052D destroyer Baotou transited near Amami Oshima off Kagoshima; PLA described as 'routine annual plan' while diplomatically protesting JMSDF transit as provocation

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW/Eastern Sectors (Apr 19, 2026)
notes: 5 PLA aircraft sorties detected; 3 of 5 entered southwestern and eastern ADIZ; 6 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship operating; Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles; activity level rising ahead of Liaoning carrier transit

label: PLA Aircraft Carrier Liaoning — Taiwan Strait Transit (Apr 20, 2026)
notes: Liaoning (CV-16) transited Taiwan Strait Apr 20, 2026; 5 PLA aircraft sorties and 12 PLAN vessels detected — highest ship count since Apr 11 post-KMT-summit surge; 4 aircraft crossed median line into SW ADIZ; Taiwan MND released aerial surveillance photographs; 25 days before Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; Liaoning is China's primary carrier training platform, commissioned 2012

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SE Sector (Apr 12, 2026)
notes: 2 PLA aircraft sorties detected as of 0600 local Apr 12, 2026; 1 of 2 sorties entered southeastern ADIZ; 8 PLAN vessels + 4 official ships (highest official ship count during KMT visit period); sharp drop from 17 sorties Apr 11 — PLA de-escalated air pressure as KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun departed Beijing for Taiwan; China simultaneously announced 10-measure cross-strait package (travel, flights, agriculture, CPC-KMT communication); Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles in response; PLA visit pattern: 1 (Apr 7) → 2 (Apr 8) → 6 (Apr 9) → 7 (Apr 10) → 17 (Apr 11) → 2 (Apr 12 — visit ends); confirms pressure-and-dialogue dual-track strategy

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — Median Line (Apr 14, 2026)
notes: 2 of 2 PLA sorties crossed the Taiwan Strait median line on April 14, 2026; 9 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship operating around Taiwan; sustained elevated naval presence 2 days post-KMT visit; consistent with PLA maintained pressure pattern preceding Trump-Xi summit (May 14-15)

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 15, 2026)
notes: 5 PLA sorties detected; 4 of 5 entered Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ on April 15, 2026; 6 PLAN vessels + 3 official ships; uptick from 2 sorties Apr 14; two days before JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi strait transit (Apr 17) which prompted China's East China Sea countermeasures on Apr 18

label: PLA ADIZ Multi-Sector Surge — 24 Sorties (Apr 21, 2026)
notes: 24 PLA aircraft sorties detected; 11 of 24 crossed median line into northern, southwestern, and eastern ADIZ simultaneously — highest non-exercise count since Justice Mission-2025 (Dec 2025); 7 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship; day after Liaoning carrier transit; two-day PLA escalation operation 23 days before Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; Taiwan deployed CAP, Navy, and coastal missiles; China MoD: activities 'safeguard national sovereignty'

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW/Eastern Sectors (Apr 22, 2026)
notes: 6 PLA aircraft sorties detected; 4 of 6 crossed Taiwan Strait median line into southwestern and eastern ADIZ sectors as of 0600 local Apr 22, 2026; 5 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship; sharp de-escalation from 24-sortie spike Apr 21; follows established spike-then-pullback pattern (Apr 11 17 sorties → Apr 12 2 sorties; Mar 29 19 sorties → Mar 30 1 sortie); 22 days to Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles in response

label: PLA ADIZ Mass Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 23, 2026 — Navy Anniversary)
notes: 14 of 15 PLA sorties entered southwestern ADIZ on PLA Navy 77th founding anniversary (Apr 23, 2026); 5 PLAN vessels + 1 official ship; re-escalation from 6-sortie pullback Apr 22; concentrated SW focus rather than multi-sector Apr 21 pattern; 21 days to Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles in response

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 24, 2026 — Post-Anniversary Pullback)
notes: 2 PLA aircraft sorties detected; 7 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan as of 0600 local Apr 24, 2026; sharp de-escalation from 15-sortie Navy anniversary spike Apr 23; 7 vessels is highest naval count since Liaoning transit Apr 20 (12 vessels); 20 days to Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; PLA calibrating air pressure while sustaining elevated maritime posture

label: PLA ADIZ Crossing — SW Sector (Apr 25, 2026 — 8 Sorties)
notes: 8 PLA aircraft sorties detected as of 0600 local Apr 25, 2026; 7 PLAN vessels stable; moderate re-escalation from 2-sortie post-anniversary lull Apr 24; PLA oscillating pattern continues 19 days before Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles in response

label: PLAN Formation 133 — Okinawa Passage (Apr 22, 2026)
notes: PLA Navy Formation 133 (Eastern Theater Command) passed through the Yonaguni-Iriomote Waterway between Japanese-administered Okinawa islands on Apr 22, 2026, returning from Western Pacific exercises; PLA characterized as 'routine far-seas training not directed at any specific country'; completes full PLA tit-for-tat response arc to JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi Taiwan Strait transit (Apr 17): East China Sea patrols (Apr 18) → Western Pacific exercises with Liaoning carrier (Apr 19-21) → Okinawa passage home (Apr 22)

label: PLA ADIZ Multi-Sector Surge — Northern/Central/SW (Apr 26, 2026 — 28 Sorties)
notes: 18 of 28 PLA sorties entered northern, central and southwestern ADIZ simultaneously on April 26, 2026 — new non-exercise peak surpassing April 21 24-sortie surge; highest non-exercise count since Justice Mission-2025 (Dec 2025); 8 PLAN vessels operating; 18 days to Trump-Xi summit May 14-15; multi-sector geometry (northern + central + SW simultaneously) matches April 21 pattern; Taiwan deployed CAP, naval ships, and coastal missiles in response; 3.5× single-day escalation from 8 sorties April 25
